pattern: Partition logs into letter-logs and digit-logs, then stable-sort letter-logs by (content, identifier) while preserving digit-log relative order.
hints: Split each log at the first space to get the identifier and body. A log is a digit-log if every character in the body is a digit or space; otherwise it is a letter-log.
Sort letter-logs with a two-part key: (body, identifier). Python's tuple sort and JavaScript's localeCompare both give the right lexicographic order.
Concatenate the sorted letter-logs followed by the digit-logs in their original order.

edge_inputs.description: only digit logs, return in original order
only letter logs, sort by content then identifier
tie on content breaks by identifier
digit-logs must keep relative order even when mixed among letter-logs
identifier that looks like a digit does not make a letter-log into a digit-log

stress.description: 50 mixed letter/digit logs with varied identifiers
